formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
Here's a list of key formulas from the chapter Ray Optics and Optical Instruments of CBSE Class 12 Physics:

[
  {
    "name": "Refraction through a Rectangular Glass Slab",
    "formula": "μ = sin i / sin r",
    "variables": "μ (Refractive Index), i (Angle of Incidence), r (Angle of Refraction)",
    "units": "No units",
    "notes": "Used to find the refractive index of a glass slab"
  },
  {
    "name": "Deviation through a Prism",
    "formula": "δ = (n – 1) A",
    "variables": "δ (Angle of Deviation), n (Refractive Index), A (Angle of the Prism)",
    "units": "Radians",
    "notes": "Used to find the angle of deviation for a prism"
  },
  {
    "name": "Total Internal Reflection",
    "formula": "sin i > sin c",
    "variables": "i (Angle of Incidence), c (Critical Angle)",
    "units": "No units",
    "notes": "Condition for total internal reflection"
  },
  {
    "name": "Magnification of a Mirror",
    "formula": "m = -v/u",
    "variables": "m (Magnification), v (Image Distance), u (Object Distance)",
    "units": "No units",
    "notes": "Used to find the magnification of a mirror"
  },
  {
    "name": "Magnification of a Lens",
    "formula": "m = -v/u",
    "variables": "m (Magnification), v (Image Distance), u (Object Distance)",
    "units": "No units",
    "notes": "Used to find the magnification of a lens"
  },
  {
    "name": "Lens Formula",
    "formula": "1/f = 1/v + 1/u",
    "variables": "f (Focal Length), v (Image Distance), u (Object Distance)",
    "units": "meters",
    "notes": "Used to find the focal length of a lens"
  },
  {
    "name": "Mirror Formula",
    "formula": "1/f = 1/v + 1/u",
    "variables": "f (Focal Length), v (Image Distance), u (Object Distance)",
    "units": "meters",
    "notes": "Used to find the focal length of a mirror"
  },
  {
    "name": "Spherical Aberration",
    "formula": "No specific formula",
    "variables": "No variables",
    "units": "No units",
    "notes": "A type of aberration that occurs in lenses and mirrors"
  }
]

Note: There are no specific formulas for this chapter. The above list includes formulas and statements that are crucial for understanding the concepts of Ray Optics and Optical Instruments.
